scenes.narration: Ye Session 2 of 10 hai Database Design series me — aaj focus: database indexing. Bhai, ek mistake jo 90% engineers karte hain — 8 second me batata hu.
Common mistake: no index samjhe bina production me lagaya — scale pe pakda gaya. Aaj ke session me exact reason aur fix dono dekhenge.
Database ek warehouse hai. Schema = shelf layout. Index = catalog. Sharding = multi-warehouse. Replication = backup branch. Transactions = receipt-trail. Sab ek ecosystem.
Core mechanism: B-tree, composite, covering index — ye sab terms aaj clear karenge with concrete examples. Yaad rakh, Database Design ka design choice = scale + cost trade-off.

Tunables: shard-key high-cardinality + uniform distribution; indexes ≤ 5 per table; replication-lag SLA <100ms; isolation level READ_COMMITTED default, REPEATABLE_READ for financial. Concepts: B-tree, composite, covering index.
Real example: Uber moved monolith Postgres → Schemaless on MySQL — sharded by trip-id. Stripe Postgres + read-replicas + Vitess sharding. Airbnb migrated MongoDB → MySQL for booking integrity.
Interview line: B-tree, composite, covering index — ye yaad rakh. Session 2/10 complete. agle session me hum database design ka next layer dekhenge.
